title: pierranthus capitatus
intro: Pierranthus capitatus, also known as the "head flower" or "yellow head," is a flowering plant that belongs to the Aizoaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Pierranthus capitatus include reducing fever, treating wounds, and relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant include making infusions by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, as well as using the plant in salves and ointments to aid in wound healing.
intro_study: According to "TheScientificWorldJournal", pierranthus capitatus (previously referred to as Phragmanthera capitata) has shown to have antibacterial activity, effectively inhibiting the growth of various gram-negative and gram-positive bacteria, with minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) ranging from 1.25 to 5 mg/mL.
